intake.user_request: Ideate how to use CODEREF-CORE to scan and document itself — a self-improving loop where the tool analyzes its own codebase, generates semantic headers, builds a RAG index of itself, and uses that index to surface improvement opportunities. Report a concrete plan before implementing anything.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Pure ideation and proposal task — no code changes, no implementation. Goal is structured analysis output: what commands to run, what queries to ask, what output to produce, and what a self-documenting workflow looks like for CODEREF-CORE specifically.

errors: layer_distribution empty: layer not propagated to element metadata (known gap from WO-LEVERAGE-AUDIT-FRESH-PASS-001 Ph5)

known_gaps: --constraint layer broken — layer not in vector metadata
stale headers: 1112 elements need --overwrite-headers to refresh
0 capability fields in element metadata — semantic headers exist but not parsed into index

in_scope: Identify what populate-coderef commands to run for self-scan
Design RAG query set to surface undocumented modules, architectural gaps, improvement opportunities, missing semantic annotations
Define what self-documenting workflow looks like for CODEREF-CORE specifically
Produce structured proposal with concrete command sequences and expected outputs
Identify which known gaps block or degrade self-scan quality
out_of_scope: Actually running the self-scan (execution is a follow-on WO)
Fixing known gaps (--constraint layer, --max-tokens) — those are separate stubs
Enabling CODEREF_LLOYD_DISCOVERY_RAG (blocked on self-scan completion per dispatch note)

constraints: Proposal only — no code changes in this WO
Must account for current known gaps in design (e.g., layer constraint broken)
Self-scan commands must work with Ollama provider (no OpenAI key)
